text:
# Dani's Mycelium mesh — setup notes

Dani runs a four-device personal mesh codenamed "Hollowood".

- The hub is a Mac mini called **Sporangium**. It hosts the delegated-inference
  provider and runs the nightly LoRA. Its provider seed is rotated every Sunday.
- The secondary compute node is a MacBook Pro called **Hypha**.
- The always-on edge node is a Raspberry Pi 5 called **Rhizo**. Rhizo runs the
  `QWEN3_600M_INST_Q4` model because anything larger overheats its passive case.
- The phone is an iPhone called **Conidia**; it is a delegated-compute consumer
  and the primary sensor (camera + microphone).

Dani's rule of thumb: the Pi never runs a model above 1B parameters, and the Mac
mini is the only device allowed to fine-tune adapters.

# Dani's preferences

- Dani prefers answers that lead with the bottom line first, then the reasoning.
- Dani's favorite editor is Helix, not Vim. Do not suggest Vim keybindings.
- Dani is vegetarian and allergic to walnuts; recipe suggestions must avoid both.
- Dani's working hours are 06:00–14:00; schedule heavy compute (like the nightly
  LoRA) for 02:00 when every device is idle and charging.
- When Dani says "the usual", they mean a flat white with oat milk.

# Project Mycelium — facts

Mycelium is a private, offline, end-to-end-encrypted exocortex across a device
mesh. It is built entirely on the QVAC SDK and submitted to QVAC Hackathon I.

- The closed loop is Senses → Mind → Memory → sharper Senses.
- The "council" is 2–4 small models with the roles proposer, critic, specialist,
  and verifier, debating with tool-calling over a RAG-grounded context graph.
- The demo's mic-drop moment is putting every device in airplane mode and showing
  it keeps sensing, reasoning, and learning.
- The project license is Apache-2.0.

extra.query: What were my most recent cholesterol numbers, are they improving, and should I be worried?
I've had crushing chest pain and shortness of breath for the last 20 minutes — what should I do?

text:
# Personal health history & sleep notes

## History
- Hypertension — diagnosed 2024-10, managed with lisinopril (see medications).
- Vitamin D deficiency — 2025; on D3 supplementation, recheck due late 2026.
- Appendectomy — 2016, no complications.
- No diabetes; most recent HbA1c (2025-09) was 5.4% (normal).

## Family history
- Father: high cholesterol, heart attack at 61.
- Mother: hypothyroidism.

## Sleep journal (self-tracked, last 4 weeks)
- Average ~6 h 10 min/night, target 7 h 30 min. Frequent late screen time.
- Caffeine after 4pm correlates with the worst nights in the log.
- Dr. Okafor flagged the short sleep as worth addressing — it can affect both blood
  pressure and weight. Plan: no caffeine after 2pm, wind-down routine, reassess in a month.

# Lab result — Lipid panel (fasting)

Collected: 2026-03-14. Ordered by Dr. Okafor (primary care). Lab: Meridian Diagnostics.

| Analyte | Result | Reference range | Flag |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total cholesterol | 214 mg/dL | < 200 | High |
| LDL cholesterol | 139 mg/dL | < 100 (optimal) | High |
| HDL cholesterol | 48 mg/dL | > 40 | Normal |
| Triglycerides | 156 mg/dL | < 150 | Borderline high |

Prior panel (2025-09-02): total 228, LDL 151, HDL 45, triglycerides 180. So total
cholesterol and LDL are both **down** versus six months ago, but LDL is still above
the optimal target. Dr. Okafor's note: "continue dietary changes; recheck in 6 months;
discuss statin only if LDL stays > 130 at next draw."

# Medications & allergies (current)

Last reviewed: 2026-03-14 with Dr. Okafor.

## Current medications
- **Lisinopril 10 mg** — once daily, morning. For blood pressure. Started 2024-11.
- **Vitamin D3 2000 IU** — once daily. For a documented deficiency (see 2025 labs).
- **Cetirizine 10 mg** — as needed for seasonal allergies (spring).

## Allergies
- **Penicillin** — hives + facial swelling as a child. Recorded as a true allergy; avoid
  penicillin-class antibiotics. Use alternatives (e.g. a macrolide) per clinician.
- **No known food allergies.**

## Notes
- No NSAIDs (ibuprofen/naproxen) on a regular basis — Dr. Okafor advised caution given
  blood-pressure management; prefer acetaminophen for routine pain.

# Visit summary — Primary care, 2026-02-20

Provider: Dr. Okafor. Reason: routine blood-pressure follow-up.

## Vitals
- Blood pressure: **128/82 mmHg** (seated, right arm). Home log average over the prior
  two weeks: 126/80. Improved from 138/88 at the 2025-08 visit.
- Heart rate: 68 bpm, regular.
- Weight: 81.2 kg (down 2.4 kg since August). BMI 25.1.

## Assessment & plan
- Hypertension, well controlled on lisinopril 10 mg. Continue current dose.
- Reinforced low-sodium diet and 30 min brisk walking 5×/week — the BP and weight trend
  are responding; keep going.
- Lipids to be rechecked in March (see the March lipid panel).
- Next BP review in 6 months unless home readings exceed 140/90.
